verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase with no transmembrane domain. It is anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) face of the plasma membrane and endosomal membranes exclusively via N-terminal myristoylation—a weak lipid interaction. All documented functions are intracellular, with SRC operating at the inner PM face to phosphorylate downstream effectors. No outer-leaflet translocation is reported in any cell state (cancer, apoptosis, activation, stress, tissue type). No contextual bucket applies: no cancer/death/activation ecto-pool; not germline/tissue-restricted; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority outer-face pool or cycling; no TM-partner tethering. The protein is absolutely inaccessible from the extracellular space.
